notes.note: The publicani (companies of tax-farmers operating in the provinces) ran their own fast courier network between Rome and the East, which private correspondents like Caelius routinely used to reach a governor.

Orestilla is probably Aurelia Orestilla, once the wife of Catiline; her daughter's marriage to young Cornificius is the kind of society news Cicero said he did not want but (Caelius teases) certainly did.

notes.anchor: she is to marry D. Brutus
notes.note: Decimus Junius Brutus Albinus, later one of Caesar's assassins; Paula Valeria's hasty divorce was arranged so she could marry him.

notes.anchor: a commander-in-chief should be obliged to ask each man in turn
notes.note: Caelius mock-relishes the picture of Cicero, saluted imperator by his troops after his Cilician campaign, having to go round asking who the adulteress was --- deflating the grand military title with low gossip.
